explanation: This command uses path normalization (C:\Users\Public\\..\Admin\AppData\Local) to reach the AppData\Local folder, which is a safe mutation technique. It also uses quotes to encapsulate the TR parameter, which is a common and safe insertion technique.

This command uses path normalization (C:\Users\Public\..\Admin\AppData\Local) and command chaining to reach the target directory. It also uses quotes and spaces to encapsulate the command, which are safe insertion techniques.

This command uses the security identifier (S-1-5-18) instead of the string 'SYSTEM', which is a substitution technique. It is functionally equivalent and avoids the keyword 'SYSTEM' that the rule is filtering on.

This command uses the /xml option to create a task from an XML file, which does not include the AppData\Local path in the command line. This is a safe omission technique as it avoids the pattern the rule is looking for.
